maml:description.maml:para: Registers an Azure Artifacts repository if needed and validates authenticated access for the selected bootstrap mode.

This cmdlet is the explicit "connect/login" companion to Register-ModuleRepository. It ensures the repository registration exists and then performs a lightweight authenticated probe so callers know whether the chosen bootstrap path can actually access the private feed.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before connecting.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before connecting.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.

command:example.maml:title: Connect to an Azure Artifacts repository using the best available bootstrap mode

maml:description.maml:para: Converts a project to a consistent encoding/line ending policy and reports the results.
This cmdlet applies a consistency policy (encoding and/or line endings) across a project tree. It can also export a post-conversion report so you can validate what remains inconsistent.
For build-time enforcement, use New-ConfigurationFileConsistency -AutoFix in the module build pipeline.
Backup root folder (mirrors the project structure).
Create backup files before modifying content.
Custom file extensions to include when ProjectType is Custom (e.g., *.ps1, *.cs).
Per-path encoding overrides (hashtable of pattern => encoding).
Ensure a final newline exists after line ending conversion.
Directory names to exclude from conversion (e.g., .git, bin, obj).
File patterns to exclude from conversion.
Export the detailed report to a CSV file at the specified path.
Convert encoding inconsistencies.
Convert line ending inconsistencies.
Force conversion even when the file already matches the target.
Per-path line ending overrides (hashtable of pattern => line ending).
Do not rollback from backup if verification mismatch occurs during encoding conversion.
Only fix files missing the final newline.
Only convert files that have mixed line endings.
Path to the project directory to convert.
Type of project to analyze. Determines which file extensions are included.
Target encoding to enforce when fixing encoding consistency.
Target line ending style to enforce when fixing line endings.
Include detailed file-by-file analysis in the output.
Source encoding filter. When Any, any non-target encoding may be converted.

maml:description.maml:para: Analyzes a script or scriptblock and reports functions/commands it calls that are not present.

This cmdlet parses PowerShell code and returns a list of referenced commands that look like missing local helpers. It is useful when building “portable” scripts/modules where you want to detect (and optionally inline) helper functions.
When -ApprovedModules is specified, helper definitions are only accepted from those modules.
Module names that are allowed sources for pulling inline helper function definitions.
Path to a script file to analyze for missing function dependencies. Alias: Path.
Known function names to treat as already available (exclude from missing list).
Function names to ignore when computing the missing set.
Return only a flattened summary list of functions used (objects with Name/Source), not inlined definitions.
Return a typed report with Summary, SummaryFiltered, and Functions.
Module names that are allowed sources for pulling inline helper function definitions.
ScriptBlock to analyze instead of a file. Alias: ScriptBlock.

Return a typed report with Summary, SummaryFiltered, and Functions.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets module manifest information from a project directory.

This is a lightweight helper used by build/publish commands. It finds the module manifest (*.psd1) under -Path and returns a structured object containing common fields such as module name, version, required modules, and the manifest path.
Use it in build scripts to avoid re-implementing manifest discovery logic.
The path to the directory containing the module manifest file.
The path to the directory containing the module manifest file.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Loads the manifest from the folder where the build script resides.

This cmdlet is designed to make CI output and local troubleshooting easier by summarizing failures from:
Use -OutputFormat to control whether the cmdlet writes a concise host summary, detailed messages, or emits JSON to the pipeline.

Include successful tests in the output (only applies to Summary format).

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the cmdlets and aliases in a .NET assembly by scanning for cmdlet-related attributes.
This is typically used by module build tooling to determine which cmdlets and aliases should be exported for binary modules (compiled cmdlets).
The cmdlet delegates scanning to a typed PowerForge service so the metadata logic can be reused outside the PowerShell surface.

maml:description.maml:para: Analyzes PowerShell files and folders to determine compatibility with Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell 7+.
Scans PowerShell files to detect patterns and constructs that can cause cross-version issues (Windows PowerShell 5.1 vs PowerShell 7+), and outputs a compatibility report.
Use this as part of CI to keep modules compatible across editions, and pair it with encoding/line-ending checks when supporting Windows PowerShell 5.1.
Directory names to exclude from analysis.
Export the detailed report to a CSV file at the specified path.
Internal mode used by build pipelines to suppress host output.
Path to the file or directory to analyze for PowerShell compatibility.
When analyzing a directory, recursively analyze all subdirectories.
Include detailed analysis of each file with specific compatibility issues found.
Directory names to exclude from analysis.
Export the detailed report to a CSV file at the specified path.
Internal mode used by build pipelines to suppress host output.
Path to the file or directory to analyze for PowerShell compatibility.
When analyzing a directory, recursively analyze all subdirectories.
Include detailed analysis of each file with specific compatibility issues found.

maml:description.maml:para: Provides comprehensive analysis of encoding and line ending consistency across a project.

This cmdlet combines encoding and line-ending analysis to provide a single “consistency” view of your repository. It is intended to be run before bulk conversions (encoding/line endings) and before packaging a module for release.
For fixing issues during builds, use New-ConfigurationFileConsistency with -AutoFix enabled.
Custom file extensions to analyze when ProjectType is Custom (e.g., *.ps1, *.cs).

Export the detailed report to a CSV file at the specified path.

Type of project to analyze. Determines which file extensions are included.

The line ending standard you want to achieve (optional).

maml:description.maml:para: Builds a .NET project in Release configuration and prepares release artefacts.

The cmdlet discovers the .csproj file (when a directory is provided), reads VersionPrefix from the project, then runs dotnet build and dotnet pack in Release (by default). It produces a ZIP snapshot of the release output and returns a typed result object for each input project path.
Use -WhatIf to preview the planned outputs without running build/pack/sign operations.
Optional certificate thumbprint used to sign assemblies and packages. When omitted, no signing is performed.
Certificate store location used when searching for the signing certificate. Default: CurrentUser.
When enabled, also packs all project dependencies that have their own .csproj files.
Path to the folder containing the project (*.csproj) file (or the csproj file itself).
Timestamp server URL used while signing. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.
Optional certificate thumbprint used to sign assemblies and packages. When omitted, no signing is performed.
Certificate store location used when searching for the signing certificate. Default: CurrentUser.
When enabled, also packs all project dependencies that have their own .csproj files.
Path to the folder containing the project (*.csproj) file (or the csproj file itself).
Timestamp server URL used while signing. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates/updates a module structure and triggers the build pipeline (legacy DSL compatible).

This is the primary entry point for building a PowerShell module using PSPublishModule. Configuration is provided via a DSL using New-Configuration* cmdlets (typically inside the -Settings scriptblock) and then executed by the PowerForge pipeline runner.
To generate a reusable powerforge.json configuration file (for the PowerForge CLI) without running any build steps, use -JsonOnly with -JsonPath.
When running in an interactive terminal, pipeline execution uses a Spectre.Console progress UI. Redirect output or use -Verbose to force plain, line-by-line output (useful for CI logs).

Dependency behavior is composed from the configuration segments you emit. Typically this means: New-ConfigurationModule declares dependencies, New-ConfigurationBuild decides whether the build host should install missing ones, and New-ConfigurationArtefact decides whether required modules should be bundled into the output artefact.
Folder name containing aliases to export. Default: Public.
Optional path to a .NET project (.csproj) to publish into the module.
Optional path to a diagnostics baseline file used to compare current issues with known issues.
Optional module roots to scan for deterministic binary conflict diagnostics. When provided, conflict findings can participate in diagnostics baselines and policy.
Build configuration for publishing the .NET project (Release or Debug).
Target frameworks to publish (e.g., net472, net8.0).
Directory names excluded from staging copy (matched by directory name, not by path).
File names excluded from staging copy (matched by file name, not by path).
Exclude patterns for artefact packaging.
When specified, requests the host to exit with code 0 on success and 1 on failure.
Fails the build when diagnostics at or above the specified severity are present.
Fails the build when diagnostics appear that are not present in the loaded baseline.
Folder name containing functions to export. Default: Public.
Writes a diagnostics baseline file from the current run.
Folders from which to include all files in artefacts.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports plugin folders from a reusable PowerForge plugin catalog configuration.

This cmdlet is the PowerShell entry point for the same generic plugin export engine used by powerforge plugin export. It plans and optionally publishes plugin project outputs into folder-based plugin bundles without hardcoding IntelligenceX-specific project lists or manifest formats into the engine.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a repository-wide .NET build/release pipeline from a JSON configuration.

The configuration file follows Schemas/project.build.schema.json in the PSPublishModule repository. Use it to define discovery rules, versioning, staging, NuGet publishing, and GitHub release settings.

GitHub tag/release templates support tokens: {Project}, {Version}, {PrimaryProject}, {PrimaryVersion}, {Repo}, {Repository}, {Date}, {UtcDate}, {DateTime}, {UtcDateTime}, {Timestamp}, {UtcTimestamp}. When GitHub release mode is Single and multiple project versions are present, {Version} defaults to the local date (yyyy.MM.dd) unless a primary project version is available.

command:example.maml:title: EXAMPLE 1

maml:description.maml:para: Tells the module to create an artefact of a specified type.
Artefacts are created after the module is built into staging. Use Packed (ZIP) for distribution and Unpacked (folder) for inspection or offline installation.

When -AddRequiredModules is enabled, required modules are copied from locally available modules (Get-Module -ListAvailable) and, when configured, downloaded (via Save-PSResource/Save-Module) before being copied into the artefact.

Only RequiredModule dependencies participate in artefact bundling. ExternalModule dependencies are intentionally excluded because they represent dependencies that should remain separately installed on the target machine.

RequiredModulesSource controls the packaging strategy: Installed means local-only copy, Auto means prefer local and download when missing, and Download means always download. When omitted, the default is Installed.
Use -ID to link an artefact to a publish step (New-ConfigurationPublish) and publish only a specific artefact.
For a broader dependency workflow explanation, see about_ModuleDependencies.
Add RequiredModule dependencies to the artefact by copying or downloading them. This does not include ExternalModule dependencies.
The name of the artefact. If not specified, the default name will be used.
Directories to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination directories should be relative to artefact root.
Files to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination files should be relative to artefact root.
Do not clear artefact output directory before creating artefact.
Enable artefact creation. By default artefact creation is disabled.
Optional ID of the artefact (to be used by New-ConfigurationPublish).
Include tag name in artefact name. By default tag name is not included.
Path where main module (or required module) will be copied to.
Path where artefact will be created.
ScriptBlock that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
ScriptBlock that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Repository credential secret (password/token) in clear text used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential secret (password/token) in a clear-text file used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential username (basic auth) used when downloading required modules.
Path where required modules will be copied to. When omitted, PowerForge uses the default module layout under the artefact output.
Repository name used when downloading required modules (Save-PSResource / Save-Module). Set this when packaging should resolve from a specific gallery or private feed.

Source used when resolving required modules (Auto / Installed / Download). When omitted, PowerForge defaults to Installed, which means packaging expects the dependency to already exist on the machine.
Tool used when downloading required modules (Save-PSResource / Save-Module). Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when necessary.
The name of the script artefact (alias: FileName).
Artefact type to generate.
Add RequiredModule dependencies to the artefact by copying or downloading them. This does not include ExternalModule dependencies.
The name of the artefact. If not specified, the default name will be used.
Directories to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination directories should be relative to artefact root.
Files to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination files should be relative to artefact root.
Do not clear artefact output directory before creating artefact.
Enable artefact creation. By default artefact creation is disabled.
Optional ID of the artefact (to be used by New-ConfigurationPublish).
Include tag name in artefact name. By default tag name is not included.
Path where main module (or required module) will be copied to.
Path where artefact will be created.
ScriptBlock that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
ScriptBlock that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Repository credential secret (password/token) in clear text used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential secret (password/token) in a clear-text file used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential username (basic auth) used when downloading required modules.

command:example.maml:title: Prefer local modules and download only when a dependency is missing

dev:remarks.maml:para: This is a good default for developer machines that may already have some dependencies installed.

maml:description.maml:para: Allows configuring the build process for a module.

This cmdlet emits build configuration that is consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. It controls how the module is merged, signed, versioned, installed, and how optional .NET publishing is performed.

Dependency-related options in this cmdlet affect the build machine, not artefact packaging. Use InstallMissingModules when the build host needs missing RequiredModule or ExternalModule dependencies installed before merge/import/test steps run.

If you want dependencies copied into ZIP/unpacked artefacts, configure that separately with New-ConfigurationArtefact -AddRequiredModules. Build-time installation and artefact packaging are designed as separate decisions because many teams want one without the other.
For a broader dependency workflow explanation, see about_ModuleDependencies.
Auto switch VersionedInstallStrategy to Exact when publishing.
Base64 string of a PFX containing a code-signing certificate.
Password for the PFX provided via CertificatePFXPath or CertificatePFXBase64.
Path to a PFX containing a code-signing certificate.
Thumbprint of a code-signing certificate from the local cert store.
Delete target module before build.
Do not attempt to fix relative paths during merge.
Keep classes in a separate dot-sourced file instead of merging into the main PSM1.
Keep library-loading code in a separate dot-sourced file.
Enable build process.
Install missing module dependencies (RequiredModule/ExternalModule) before build. This affects the build host only; it does not bundle modules into artefacts.
Credential secret or token for dependency installation. Prefer the file-path form in CI when you do not want the secret value embedded directly in scripts.
Path to a file containing the credential secret or token. This is often the safest option for automation and CI agents.
Credential user name for dependency installation. This is usually paired with InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecret or InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecretFilePath.
Force re-install or update even if dependencies are already installed. Useful when you want the build host to re-sync against the repository instead of accepting the current local state.
Allow prerelease versions when installing dependencies. Use this only when the dependency declaration and repository policy intentionally allow prerelease packages.
Repository name used for dependency installation (defaults to PSGallery). Set this when your build should resolve dependencies from a named private feed or alternate gallery.
Kill locking processes before install.
Force killing locking processes before install.
Use local versioning (bump PSD1 version on each build without querying PSGallery).
When merging, also include functions from ApprovedModules referenced by the module.
Merge module on build (combine Private/Public/Classes/Enums into one PSM1).
Binary module names (DLL file names) to import in the module.
Disable cmdlet scanning for the binary module.
Enable binary module documentation.
Build configuration for .NET projects (Release or Debug).
Do not copy libraries recursively (legacy option).
Filters for libraries that should be excluded from build output.
Exclude main library from build output.
Target frameworks for .NET build.
Handle 'assembly with same name is already loaded' by wrapping Add-Type logic.
Handle runtimes folder when copying libraries.
Exclude libraries from being loaded by PSM1/Libraries.ps1.
Add-Type libraries line by line (legacy debugging option).
Debug DLL merge (legacy setting).
Project name for the .NET project (required when NETProjectPath is provided).
Path to the .NET project to build (useful when not in Sources folder).
Enable resolving binary conflicts.
Project name used when resolving binary conflicts.
Search class (legacy option).
Only regenerate the manifest (PSD1) without rebuilding/merging other artefacts.
Resolve Auto/Latest dependency versions from the repository without installing. When not explicitly set, this is auto-enabled if any RequiredModules use Auto/Latest/Guid Auto.
Write library-loading code into a distinct file and reference it via ScriptsToProcess/DotSource.
Override include patterns passed to the signer.
Additional path substrings to exclude from signing.
When signing is enabled, binaries are signed by default (e.g., .dll, .cat). Use -SignIncludeBinaries:$false to opt out.
When signing is enabled, include .exe files in signing.
When signing is enabled, also sign scripts that reside under the Internals folder.
Enable code-signing for the built module output.
When signing is enabled, overwrite existing signatures (re-sign files).
Disables built-in replacements done by the module builder.
Synchronize the source .NET project version with the resolved module/manifest version before staging. This is opt-in and updates the source .csproj file when a project path can be resolved.
Export all functions/aliases via wildcard in PSD1.
How many versions to keep per module when using versioned installs.
How to handle legacy flat module installs during install.
Version folders to preserve during install pruning (for example older major versions).
Controls how the module is installed into user Module roots after build.
Warn if RequiredModule entries are older than the latest version available in the repository. This is a reporting hint and does not change the manifest or install anything by itself.

Credential secret or token for dependency installation. Prefer the file-path form in CI when you do not want the secret value embedded directly in scripts.
Path to a file containing the credential secret or token. This is often the safest option for automation and CI agents.
Credential user name for dependency installation. This is usually paired with InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecret or InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecretFilePath.
Force re-install or update even if dependencies are already installed. Useful when you want the build host to re-sync against the repository instead of accepting the current local state.
Allow prerelease versions when installing dependencies. Use this only when the dependency declaration and repository policy intentionally allow prerelease packages.
Repository name used for dependency installation (defaults to PSGallery). Set this when your build should resolve dependencies from a named private feed or alternate gallery.
Kill locking processes before install.
Force killing locking processes before install.
Use local versioning (bump PSD1 version on each build without querying PSGallery).
When merging, also include functions from ApprovedModules referenced by the module.
Merge module on build (combine Private/Public/Classes/Enums into one PSM1).
Binary module names (DLL file names) to import in the module.
Disable cmdlet scanning for the binary module.
Enable binary module documentation.
Build configuration for .NET projects (Release or Debug).
Do not copy libraries recursively (legacy option).
Filters for libraries that should be excluded from build output.
Exclude main library from build output.
Target frameworks for .NET build.
Handle 'assembly with same name is already loaded' by wrapping Add-Type logic.
Handle runtimes folder when copying libraries.
Exclude libraries from being loaded by PSM1/Libraries.ps1.
Add-Type libraries line by line (legacy debugging option).
Debug DLL merge (legacy setting).
Project name for the .NET project (required when NETProjectPath is provided).
Path to the .NET project to build (useful when not in Sources folder).
Enable resolving binary conflicts.
Project name used when resolving binary conflicts.
Search class (legacy option).
Only regenerate the manifest (PSD1) without rebuilding/merging other artefacts.
Resolve Auto/Latest dependency versions from the repository without installing. When not explicitly set, this is auto-enabled if any RequiredModules use Auto/Latest/Guid Auto.
Write library-loading code into a distinct file and reference it via ScriptsToProcess/DotSource.
Override include patterns passed to the signer.
Additional path substrings to exclude from signing.
When signing is enabled, binaries are signed by default (e.g., .dll, .cat). Use -SignIncludeBinaries:$false to opt out.
When signing is enabled, include .exe files in signing.
When signing is enabled, also sign scripts that reside under the Internals folder.
Enable code-signing for the built module output.
When signing is enabled, overwrite existing signatures (re-sign files).
Disables built-in replacements done by the module builder.
Synchronize the source .NET project version with the resolved module/manifest version before staging. This is opt-in and updates the source .csproj file when a project path can be resolved.
Export all functions/aliases via wildcard in PSD1.
How many versions to keep per module when using versioned installs.
How to handle legacy flat module installs during install.
Version folders to preserve during install pruning (for example older major versions).
Controls how the module is installed into user Module roots after build.
Warn if RequiredModule entries are older than the latest version available in the repository. This is a reporting hint and does not change the manifest or install anything by itself.

maml:description.maml:para: Configures delivery metadata for bundling and installing internal docs/examples.

Delivery configuration is used to bundle “internals” (docs, examples, tools, configuration files) into a module and optionally generate public helper commands (Install-<ModuleName> / Update-<ModuleName>) that can copy these files to a target folder.
This is intended for “script packages” where the module contains additional artifacts that should be deployed alongside it.
Merge behavior for generated delivery commands can be fine-tuned with PreservePaths and OverwritePaths so selected relative paths keep local changes or are refreshed during updates.
Where to bundle CHANGELOG.* within the built module.
Optional file-name order for Internals\\Docs when rendering documentation.
Enables delivery metadata emission.
When set, generates a public Install-<ModuleName> helper function during build that copies Internals to a destination folder.
When set, generates a public Update-<ModuleName> helper function during build that delegates to the install command.
Important links (Title/Url). Accepts legacy hashtable array (@{ Title='..'; Url='..' }) or DeliveryImportantLink[].
Include module root CHANGELOG.* during installation.
Include module root LICENSE.* during installation.
Include module root README.* during installation.
Optional override name for the generated install command. When empty, defaults to Install-<ModuleName>.
Relative path inside the module that contains internal deliverables.
Relative path (within the module root) to a Markdown/text file to use as Intro content.
Text lines shown to users after Install-ModuleDocumentation completes.
Where to bundle LICENSE.* within the built module.
Optional wildcard patterns (relative to Internals) that should be overwritten during merge installs by generated Install-/Update- helpers. Example: Artefacts/**.
Optional wildcard patterns (relative to Internals) that should be preserved during merge installs by generated Install-/Update- helpers. Example: Config/**.
Where to bundle README.* within the built module.
Optional branch name to use when fetching remote documentation.
One or more repository-relative paths from which to display remote documentation files.
When set, requests signing for files under InternalsPath using the configured module signing settings/certificate.
Optional override name for the generated update command. When empty, defaults to Update-<ModuleName>.
Relative path (within the module root) to a Markdown/text file to use for Upgrade instructions.
Text lines with upgrade instructions shown when requested.
Where to bundle CHANGELOG.* within the built module.
Optional file-name order for Internals\\Docs when rendering documentation.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables creation of documentation from the module using PowerForge.

This cmdlet emits documentation configuration segments that are consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. It controls markdown generation (in -Path), optional external help generation (MAML, e.g. en-US\<ModuleName>-help.xml), and whether generated documentation should be synced back to the project root.

About topics are supported via about_*.help.txt / about_*.txt / about_*.md / about_*.markdown files present in the module source. When enabled, these are converted into markdown pages under Docs\About. Additional source roots can be provided via AboutTopicsSourcePath.
Optional extra source paths for about_* topic files (.help.txt, .txt, .md, .markdown). Relative paths are resolved from the staging root (for example: 'Help\About').
Enables creation of documentation from the module.
Culture folder for generated external help (default: en-US).
Optional file name override for external help (default: <ModuleName>-help.xml).
Path to the folder where documentation will be created.
Path to the readme file that will be used for the documentation.
Disable conversion of about_* topics into markdown pages.
Disable external help (MAML) generation.
Disable generating basic fallback examples for cmdlets missing examples.
Removes all files from the documentation folder before creating new documentation.
When enabled and UpdateWhenNew is set, the generated external help file is also synced back to the project root (e.g. en-US\<ModuleName>-help.xml).
Documentation engine (legacy parameter; kept for compatibility).
When enabled, generated documentation is also synced back to the project folder (not only to the staging build output).
Optional extra source paths for about_* topic files (.help.txt, .txt, .md, .markdown). Relative paths are resolved from the staging root (for example: 'Help\About').

When enabled and UpdateWhenNew is set, the generated external help file is also synced back to the project root (e.g. en-US\<ModuleName>-help.xml).
Documentation engine (legacy parameter; kept for compatibility).
When enabled, generated documentation is also synced back to the project folder (not only to the staging build output).

maml:description.maml:para: Provides a way to configure required, external, or approved modules used in the project.
Emits module dependency configuration segments. These are later used to patch the module manifest and (optionally) install/package dependencies during a build.

Use RequiredModule for dependencies that should appear in the manifest and can also be bundled into build artefacts when New-ConfigurationArtefact -AddRequiredModules is enabled. Use ExternalModule for dependencies that must exist on the target system but should not be bundled into artefacts.

RequiredModule entries are written to the manifest RequiredModules. ExternalModule entries are written to PrivateData.PSData.ExternalModuleDependencies. ApprovedModule entries are used by merge/missing-function workflows and are not emitted as manifest dependencies.
Built-in Microsoft.PowerShell.* modules are ignored during manifest refresh because they are inbox runtime modules, not gallery-resolvable dependencies.

Version and Guid values set to Auto or Latest are resolved from installed modules by default. When New-ConfigurationBuild -ResolveMissingModulesOnline is enabled, repository results can be used without installing the dependency first.
Choose only one versioning style per dependency: a minimum version (-Version or -MinimumVersion) or an exact version (-RequiredVersion). Mixing them for the same module is treated as invalid input.
GUID of the dependency module (or Auto). This is most useful when you want manifest validation to lock onto a specific module identity across repositories.
Minimum version of the dependency module (preferred over -Version). Use this when any newer compatible version is acceptable.
Name of the PowerShell module(s) that your module depends on. Multiple names emit one configuration segment per module using the same dependency settings.
Required version of the dependency module (exact match). Use this when consumers and packaging must resolve the exact same version.
Choose between RequiredModule, ExternalModule, and ApprovedModule. RequiredModule is used for manifest and optional packaging, ExternalModule is install-only, and ApprovedModule is merge-only.
Minimum version of the dependency module (or Auto/Latest). This is treated the same as -MinimumVersion and cannot be combined with -RequiredVersion.
GUID of the dependency module (or Auto). This is most useful when you want manifest validation to lock onto a specific module identity across repositories.
Minimum version of the dependency module (preferred over -Version). Use this when any newer compatible version is acceptable.
Name of the PowerShell module(s) that your module depends on. Multiple names emit one configuration segment per module using the same dependency settings.
Required version of the dependency module (exact match). Use this when consumers and packaging must resolve the exact same version.
Choose between RequiredModule, ExternalModule, and ApprovedModule. RequiredModule is used for manifest and optional packaging, ExternalModule is install-only, and ApprovedModule is merge-only.
Minimum version of the dependency module (or Auto/Latest). This is treated the same as -MinimumVersion and cannot be combined with -RequiredVersion.

maml:description.maml:para: Provides a way to ignore certain commands or modules during build process and continue module building on errors.

Force build process to continue even if the module or command is not available.
Ignore function name(s). If the function is not available it will be ignored.
Ignore module name(s). If the module is not available it will be ignored.
Fail build when unresolved commands are detected during merge.
Force build process to continue even if the module or command is not available.
Ignore function name(s). If the function is not available it will be ignored.
Ignore module name(s). If the module is not available it will be ignored.

maml:description.maml:para: Provides a way to configure publishing to PowerShell Gallery, GitHub, or private galleries such as Azure Artifacts.

This cmdlet emits publish configuration consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. Use -Type to choose a destination. For repository publishing, -Tool selects the provider (PowerShellGet/PSResourceGet/Auto).

For private repositories (for example Azure DevOps Artifacts / private NuGet v3 feeds), provide repository URIs and (optionally) credentials, or use the Azure Artifacts preset parameters to resolve those URIs automatically. To avoid secrets in source control, pass API keys/tokens via -FilePath or environment-specific tooling.
Publish GitHub release as a release even if module prerelease is set.
Enable publishing to the chosen destination.
When true, registers/updates the repository before publishing. Default: true.
API key to be used for publishing in clear text in a file.
Allow publishing lower version of a module on a PowerShell repository.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically.
Optional ID of the artefact used for publishing.
Override tag name used for GitHub publishing.
Repository API version for PSResourceGet registration (v2/v3).
Repository credential secret (password/token) in clear text.
Repository credential secret (password/token) in a clear-text file.
Repository credential username (basic auth).
Repository name override (GitHub or PowerShell repository name).
Repository priority for PSResourceGet (lower is higher priority).
Repository publish URI (PowerShellGet PublishLocation).
Repository source URI (PowerShellGet SourceLocation).
Whether to mark the repository as trusted (avoids prompts). Default: true.
Repository base URI (used for both source and publish unless overridden).
Publishing tool/provider used for repository publishing. Ignored for GitHub publishing.
Choose between PowerShellGallery and GitHub.
When set, unregisters the repository after publish if it was created by this run.

maml:description.maml:para: Publishes a release asset to GitHub (creates a release and uploads a zip).
Reads project metadata from *.csproj, resolves the release version (unless overridden), creates a GitHub release, and uploads the specified ZIP asset.
For private repositories, use a token with the minimal required scope and prefer providing it via an environment variable.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically.
Personal access token used for authentication.
Name of the GitHub repository.
GitHub account name owning the repository.
When set, generates tag name as <Project>-v<Version>.
Publish the release as a pre-release.
Path to the project folder containing the *.csproj file.
Optional release name override (defaults to TagName).
Optional tag name override.
Optional tag template (supports {Project} and {Version}).
Optional version override (otherwise read from VersionPrefix).
Optional zip path override (defaults to bin/Release/<Project>.<Version>.zip).
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically.
Personal access token used for authentication.
Name of the GitHub repository.
GitHub account name owning the repository.
When set, generates tag name as <Project>-v<Version>.
Publish the release as a pre-release.
Path to the project folder containing the *.csproj file.
Optional release name override (defaults to TagName).
Optional tag name override.
Optional tag template (supports {Project} and {Version}).
Optional version override (otherwise read from VersionPrefix).
Optional zip path override (defaults to bin/Release/<Project>.<Version>.zip).

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a GitHub release and uploads bin\Release\<Project>.<Version>.zip.

maml:description.maml:para: Pushes NuGet packages to a feed using dotnet nuget push.
Searches the provided -Path roots for *.nupkg files and pushes them using the .NET SDK.
Use -SkipDuplicate for CI-friendly, idempotent runs.
API key used to authenticate against the NuGet feed.
Directory to search for NuGet packages.
When set, passes --skip-duplicate to dotnet nuget push. This makes repeated publishing runs idempotent when the package already exists.
NuGet feed URL.
API key used to authenticate against the NuGet feed.
Directory to search for NuGet packages.
When set, passes --skip-duplicate to dotnet nuget push. This makes repeated publishing runs idempotent when the package already exists.
NuGet feed URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Signs files in a path using a code-signing certificate (Windows and PowerShell Core supported).
Signs PowerShell scripts/manifests (and optionally binaries) using Authenticode. When running in CI, prefer using a certificate from the Windows certificate store and referencing it by thumbprint.
One or more path substrings to exclude from signing.
Hash algorithm used for the signature. Default: SHA256.
File patterns to include during signing. Default: scripts only.
Which portion of the chain to include in the signature. Default: All.
Certificate store to search when using a certificate from the store.
Root directory containing files to sign.
Certificate thumbprint to select a single certificate from the chosen store.
RFC3161 timestamp server URL. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.
A PFX file to use for signing (mutually exclusive with -LocalStore/-Thumbprint).

RFC3161 timestamp server URL. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.

Which portion of the chain to include in the signature. Default: All.

Certificate thumbprint to select a single certificate from the chosen store.

maml:description.maml:para: Registers an Azure Artifacts feed as a private PowerShell module repository for PowerShellGet and/or PSResourceGet.
This cmdlet simplifies Azure Artifacts setup on end-user machines by resolving the correct v2/v3 endpoints and registering the repository for the selected client tools.

For PowerShellGet, supplied credentials are forwarded to Register-PSRepository so later Install-Module calls can reuse the registered source. For PSResourceGet, the repository is registered with the Azure Artifacts v3 endpoint so Install-PSResource can use the Azure Artifacts credential provider.

The output object indicates which native install paths are ready after registration, so callers can see whether Install-PSResource, Install-Module, or both are available for the configured repository.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before registration.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before registration.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes PowerShell comments from a script file or provided content, with optional empty-line normalization.
Uses the PowerShell parser (AST) to remove comments safely rather than relying on fragile regex-only approaches. Useful as a preprocessing step when producing merged/packed scripts.

Do not remove a signature block, if present.
Remove all empty lines from the content.
Remove comments before the param block. By default comments before the param block are not removed.
Remove comments in the param block. By default comments in the param block are not removed.
Remove empty lines if more than one empty line is found.

Remove all empty lines from the content.

Remove empty lines if more than one empty line is found.
File path to the source file.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes specific files and folders from a project directory with safety features.
Designed for build/CI cleanup scenarios where removing generated artifacts (bin/obj, packed outputs, temporary files) should be predictable and safe. Supports -WhatIf, retries and optional backups.

Method to use for deletion.

Return detailed results.

Type of project cleanup to perform.
Process subdirectories recursively. Defaults to true unless explicitly specified.
Number of retry attempts for each deletion.
Display progress information during cleanup.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new release for the given GitHub repository and optionally uploads assets.

This cmdlet uses the GitHub REST API to create a release and upload assets. It is a lower-level building block used by higher-level helpers (such as Publish-GitHubReleaseAsset) and can also be used directly in CI pipelines.
Provide the token via an environment variable to avoid leaking secrets into logs or history.
The full paths of the files to include as release assets.
Commitish value that determines where the Git tag is created from.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically (cannot be used with ReleaseNotes).
GitHub personal access token used for authentication.
GitHub repository name.
GitHub username owning the repository.
True to create a draft (unpublished) release.
True to identify the release as a prerelease.
The name of the release. If omitted, TagName is used.
The text describing the contents of the release.
When true (default), a 422 tag conflict reuses the existing release. When false, the cmdlet fails on existing tags.
The tag name used for the release.
The full paths of the files to include as release assets.
Commitish value that determines where the Git tag is created from.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically (cannot be used with ReleaseNotes).
GitHub personal access token used for authentication.
GitHub repository name.
GitHub username owning the repository.
True to create a draft (unpublished) release.
True to identify the release as a prerelease.
The name of the release. If omitted, TagName is used.
The text describing the contents of the release.
When true (default), a 422 tag conflict reuses the existing release. When false, the cmdlet fails on existing tags.
The tag name used for the release.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates version numbers across multiple project files.
Updates version numbers in: C# projects (*.csproj)PowerShell module manifests (*.psd1)PowerShell build scripts that reference Invoke-ModuleBuild
Use -VersionType to increment one component, or -NewVersion to set an explicit version.
Path fragments (or folder names) to exclude from the search (in addition to default 'obj' and 'bin'). This matches against the full path, case-insensitively.
Optional module name to filter updates to specific projects/modules.
Specific version number to set (format: x.x.x or x.x.x.x).
Returns per-file update results when specified.
The root path to search for project files. Defaults to current directory.
The type of version increment: Major, Minor, Build, or Revision.

maml:description.maml:para: Steps a version based on an expected version pattern (supports the legacy X placeholder).
This cmdlet supports two common workflows:
When -ExpectedVersion contains an X placeholder (e.g. 1.2.X), the cmdlet resolves the next patch version. When an exact version is provided, it is returned as-is.
When set, returns a typed result instead of only the version string.
Expected version (exact or pattern like 0.1.X).
Optional local PSD1 path used to resolve current version.
Optional module name used to resolve current version from PSGallery.
When set, returns a typed result instead of only the version string.
Expected version (exact or pattern like 0.1.X).
Optional local PSD1 path used to resolve current version.
Optional module name used to resolve current version from PSGallery.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns a structured object that includes whether auto-versioning was used.

maml:description.maml:para: Refreshes or repairs an Azure Artifacts private PowerShell module repository registration.

This cmdlet uses the same repository resolution logic as Register-ModuleRepository, but is intended for reconnecting or repairing repository configuration after credentials, endpoints, or tool-specific registration details have changed.
The output object indicates which native install paths are ready after refresh, so callers can see whether Install-PSResource, Install-Module, or both are available for the configured repository.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before refresh.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.
Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Bootstrap/authentication mode. Auto uses supplied or prompted credentials when requested; otherwise it prefers ExistingSession when Azure Artifacts prerequisites are ready and falls back to CredentialPrompt when they are not.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before refresh.
Optional repository name override. Defaults to the feed name.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.
Registration strategy. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted.

Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Module names to update.
Includes prerelease versions when supported by the selected installer.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.

Azure Artifacts feed name.
Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.

Optional repository credential username.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites such as PSResourceGet and the Azure Artifacts credential provider before automatic registration refresh.

Includes prerelease versions when supported by the selected installer.
Optional PSResourceGet repository priority used during automatic registration refresh.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials.
Private gallery provider. Currently only AzureArtifacts is supported.

Registration strategy used when Azure Artifacts details are supplied. Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when needed.
When true, marks the repository as trusted during automatic registration refresh.

Azure DevOps organization name.
Optional Azure DevOps project name for project-scoped feeds.
